title: A soul place!
text: Not for the feint hearted! This is hard-core off-road camping at its best. You need to be 100% self-sufficient; take what you need or do without. If you want game and birding around every corner then go somewhere else. That said, for spiritual peace… calm and an island in a busy world, this is a soul rejuvenating experience.
original_text: Not for the feint hearted! This is hard-core off-road camping at its best. You need to be 100% self-sufficient; take what you need or do without. If you want game and birding around every corner then go somewhere else. That said, for spiritual peace… calm and an island in a busy world, this is a soul rejuvenating experience.

title: Wild road,wild animals and wild nature
text: Incredible nature and animal experience. Camping at wild locations where animals walk through the camp. Getting there is a real challenge and only 4x4 accessible, but it’s worth it.
original_title: Wild road,wild animals and wild nature
original_text: Incredible nature and animal experience. Camping at wild locations where animals walk through the camp. Getting there is a real challenge and only 4x4 accessible, but it’s worth it.

title: Disappointing experience
text: Central Kalahari Game Reserve is a vast and deserted place, not for beginners! It is the world's second largest national park, about the size of Denmark. We spent three days/2 nights in there and saw only one other vehicle. In some places you can be lucky to see animals, we only saw a couple of elephant and a few antelopes and birds. There should be good chances to see lions, but we saw or heard none. The facilities at the public camp sites (which must be booked in advance - not cheap) are shaded, simple and run down. There is a fireplace, a longdrop toilet and a "shower", where you must bring your own water and put it in a bucket! Considering the exorbitant entrance fees to the park, there should be a little left over to update the park. The tracks in the park are sandy and difficult, the thorny shrubs along the tracks can give heavy scratches to the vehicles at extra cost to the rental company. Signs are worn off and impossible to read, or non existent. It is easy to get lost! Don't go in one vehicle only, go as a group of cars, if you must. We entered at Tsau Gate, near Kuke veterinarian fence, and it is a fairly long drive in loose sand before you reach the gate - and what a gate. The once beautiful traditional thatched entrance buildings are totally dilapidated - the roof gone, the toilets broken - a depressing sight. Instead an expensive tent structure serves as the office. Shame.
